title: Relative Telomere Length Measurement
steps: GenomicDNA was extracted using the DNeasy Blood and Tissue Kit (Qiagen, Hilden, Germany

10 ng/μL DNA was amplified in a 5x EvaGreen‱ mix (No ROX) (Bio&Sell, Nurnberg, Germany). The thermal cycling protocol of Telomere A: 5′-CGGTTTGTTTGGGTTTGGGTTTGGGTTTGGGTTTGGGTT-3′, Telomere B: 5′-GGCTTGCCTTACCCTTACCCTTACCCTTACCCTTACCCT-3′ and B2M was: 95°C for 15 min, followed by 40 cycles of 95°C for 15 s, 56°C for 20 s, and 72°C for20 s. The thermal cycling protocol of Telomere Primer Pair 1 was:95°C for 15 min, followed by 2 cycles of 15 s at 94°C, 1 cycle of 15 s at 49°C,40 cycles of 15 s at 94°C, 1 cycle 10 s at 62°C, 1 cycle 15 s at 74°C with signal acquisition, 10 s at 84°C, and 15 s at 88°C with signal acquisition. A no-template control (NTC) and the all templates were run in triplicate for each reaction

Primer sequences Relative telomere length (RTL) was determined using two different primer pairs for telomere amplification and a primer pair for the single-copy reference gene beta-2-microglobulin(B2M) . The following primers were employed: Telomere Primer Pair 1 (Hermann C, 2023): Telg: 5′-ACACTAAGGTTTGGGTTTGGGTTTGGGTTTGGGTTAGTGT-3′,Telc:5′-TGTTAGGTATCCCTATCCCTATCCCTATCCCTATCCCTAACA-3′. Telomere Primer Pair 2 (Mugdha, 2020): Telomere A: 5′-CGGTTTGTTTGGGTTTGGGTTTGGGTTTGGGTTTGGGTT-3′, Telomere B: 5′-GGCTTGCCTTACCCTTACCCTTACCCTTACCCTTACCCT-3′. Single Copy Gene: Beta-2-microglobulin (B2M): Forward (F): 5′-CAAGACACCCGCCAGAAGAT-3′, Reverse (R): 5′-CAGCGTGGGACAGAAGGTAG-3′ (Designed by our lab).
Data were analyzed using ViiA7 software (ThermoFisher, MA, USA), and RTL was calculated using the ΔΔCt method: ΔΔCt = (Sample B2M Ct - Sample Telomere Ct) - (Control B2M Ct - Control Telomere Ct).
creator: Karyna Tarasova
